entities.name: WILD SORGHUM
entities.description: Wild sorghum (Sorghum dimidiatum Stapf.), the target species for plant regeneration.

entities.name: PLANT REGENERATION
entities.description: The process of regenerating whole plants from cell or tissue cultures.

entities.name: CELL SUSPENSION CULTURES
entities.description: Cultures of cells suspended in a liquid medium used for plant regeneration.

entities.name: SHOOT-MERISTEM-DERIVED CALLUS
entities.description: Callus tissue derived from the shoot meristem used to establish cell suspensions.

entities.name: MURASHIGE AND SKOOG AGAR MEDIUM
entities.description: A standard plant tissue culture medium used for plating cell suspensions.

entities.name: 2,4-DICHLOROPHEN-OXYACETIC ACID
entities.description: A plant growth regulator (auxin) used to induce embryogenic calli and maintain cultures.

entities.name: EMBRYOGENIC CALLI
entities.description: Callus tissue capable of producing somatic embryos.

entities.name: SOMATIC EMBRYOGENESIS
entities.description: The process of forming embryos from somatic cells.

entities.name: REGENERATION MEDIUM
entities.description: A medium used to induce the development of embryos and plantlets.

entities.name: KINETIN
entities.description: A cytokinin used in the regeneration medium to promote plantlet development.

entities.name: 6-BENZYLAMINOPURINE
entities.description: A cytokinin used in the regeneration medium to promote plantlet development.

entities.name: SOMATIC EMBRYOS
entities.description: Embryos derived from somatic cells that can develop into plants.

entities.name: PLANTLETS
entities.description: Small, young plants regenerated from somatic embryos.
